objective: Design the first high-value BirdCLEF 2026 experiment queue using the current harness state, emphasizing fast score gains, ablation value, and practical leaderboard pressure before the 2026-06-03 deadline.
success_criteria: Rank the first 8-12 experiments by expected information gain and expected leaderboard value.
Do not assume any currently coded baseline is already the active anchor; choose the anchor explicitly from the candidate method manifests and justify it.
Identify which experiments are pure baselines, which are ablations, and which are exploitation-oriented leaderboard pushes.
Produce explicit stop conditions and success thresholds for each experiment.
evidence_requirements: Each queued run must include why it matters for leaderboard score rather than only offline neatness.
Every external-data or pseudo-labeling recommendation must respect the current external-data policy.
If a recommendation depends on assumptions about the competition metric, those assumptions must be stated explicitly.
Separate directly verified facts from working assumptions.
stop_conditions: The metric definition remains too ambiguous to prioritize threshold-sensitive experiments.
The current evidence is too weak to justify naming an active baseline.
The queue would spend A100 time before the submission loop is functional.
